title: shadcn/ui Conventions
hook: shadcn is 'right' — but only if you use it right.
description: Cursor rules for using shadcn/ui correctly — which primitives to compose, how to override via className without breaking variants, how to theme via CSS variables, accessibility patterns, and why you shouldn't abstract shadcn components into your own wrappers.
previewContent:
```mdc
---
description: shadcn/ui composition rules
globs: '**/components/**/*.tsx'
alwaysApply: false
---

- Compose shadcn primitives. Do not wrap them in your own component unless you're adding real behavior.
- Override via className + cn() helper — not via style props.
- Use Radix accessibility out of the box — don't re-implement focus management.
- Theme via CSS variables in globals.css, not JS theme objects.
```
body:
# shadcn/ui Conventions

shadcn/ui is a component library where you own the source code. That's the feature. It also means the most common mistake is to wrap every primitive in a project-specific component, losing the benefit.

## The core principle

**Compose shadcn primitives directly. Do not wrap them unless you're adding real behavior.**

Bad:
```tsx
// components/MyButton.tsx — useless wrapper
export function MyButton(props) {
  return <Button {...props} />;
}
```

Good:
```tsx
// In your feature code, use shadcn Button directly
<Button variant="outline" size="sm">Save</Button>
```

Good (when you're actually adding behavior):
```tsx
// A Button that shows loading spinner + disables itself
export function LoadingButton({ loading, children, ...props }) {
  return (
    <Button {...props} disabled={loading || props.disabled}>
      {loading && <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />}
      {children}
    </Button>
  );
}
```

## Rule 1: composition-over-wrapping.mdc

```mdc
---
description: shadcn composition
globs: '**/components/**/*.tsx'
alwaysApply: false
---

- Use shadcn primitives directly in feature code. No MyButton, MyCard, MyInput wrappers.
- Create wrappers only when they add real behavior: loading states, permission gates, compound components (CardWithHeader, DialogWithActions).
- If you find yourself copying `<Button variant="outline" size="sm" className="w-full">` ten times, that's a signal to create a compound component, not a wrapper.
- Never re-export shadcn primitives from your own component library. That breaks the shadcn update flow and makes upstream fixes invisible.
```

## Rule 2: className-overrides.mdc

```mdc
---
description: Using className and cn() for shadcn overrides
globs: '**'
alwaysApply: false
---

- All shadcn primitives accept `className`. Use it for project-specific tweaks.
- Always merge with `cn()` helper (from @/lib/utils). Never concatenate strings directly — you'll lose Tailwind's conflict resolution.
- Tailwind utilities applied via className take precedence over the primitive's defaults because shadcn uses `cva()` with the className as the last argument.
- Don't use `style={{...}}` for layout or color. That defeats theming.
- Do use `style` for one-off dynamic values (gradient angles, animation timings, computed positions).
```

## Rule 3: theming-via-css-variables.mdc

```mdc
---
description: shadcn theming
globs: '**/globals.css,**/tailwind.config.*'
alwaysApply: false
---

- Theme lives in globals.css as CSS variables: --background, --foreground, --primary, etc.
- Dark mode uses .dark class on html (shadcn default).
- To add a brand color, define it as a CSS variable with HSL triples (e.g., `--brand: 45 100% 70%`) then reference it in tailwind.config.ts via `hsl(var(--brand))`.
- Never hardcode colors in components. Always reference the semantic token.
- Don't add more than 12 semantic tokens. If you're past that, your design system is sprawling.
```

## Rule 4: accessibility-out-of-the-box.mdc

```mdc
---
description: shadcn accessibility
globs: '**/components/**/*.tsx'
alwaysApply: false
---

- shadcn builds on Radix UI. Dialog, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, etc. handle focus management, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels automatically. Do not reimplement.
- Always use Radix primitives via their shadcn wrappers, not the bare Radix imports. The wrappers include the styling.
- For custom interactive elements, check if Radix has a primitive first. It usually does.
- Icon-only buttons still need aria-label: `<Button aria-label="Close menu"><X /></Button>`.
- Form inputs need labeled via <Label htmlFor={id}> or aria-labelledby.
- Never remove focus rings. If they look ugly, restyle them via the --ring token, don't delete them.
```

## Rule 5: compound-components.mdc

```mdc
---
description: Compound component patterns with shadcn
globs: '**/components/**/*.tsx'
alwaysApply: false
---

- shadcn primitives often come as compound components: Card + CardHeader + CardTitle + CardDescription + CardContent + CardFooter. Use them as a set.
- Don't skip the semantic structure. A Card without a CardHeader is weaker accessibility.
- If you need a variant of a compound component (e.g., a "featured" Card), create a wrapper that composes all the parts with the right props.
- Never export individual parts of a compound component in isolation. They belong together.
```

## Rule 6: update-flow.mdc

```mdc
---
description: Updating shadcn components
globs: '**/components/ui/**'
alwaysApply: false
---

- shadcn is distributed as source code. When they update a component, you need to re-run `npx shadcn@latest add <component>`.
- Before updating, diff the new version against your customized version. Carry your customizations forward manually.
- Keep customizations SMALL. Big customizations lock you out of updates.
- Document any customizations with a comment: `// shadcn: customized {reason} — review on update`.
- Pin shadcn CLI version in your README so team updates are coordinated.
```
